description: You are a ground station operator tasked with monitoring the RF link stability of the INST satellite. Write a real-time monitoring script in OpenC3 COSMOS to quantify data loss during a signal outage. The script must monitor INST HEALTH_STATUS telemetry for at least 45 seconds, identifying gaps where telemetry values do not update for strictly more than 3.0 seconds. While the script is running, execute the simulator at /home/ga/Desktop/simulate_link_drop.sh to trigger a randomized signal outage. Calculate the total monitoring duration, the number of read cycles performed, the number of gaps detected, and the duration of the largest gap in seconds. Save the results to /home/ga/Desktop/gap_report.json with the following keys: 'monitoring_duration_seconds', 'total_read_cycles', 'gaps_detected', and 'largest_gap_seconds'. OpenC3 password: Cosmos2024!
